807. If \(x=\dfrac{y+(n-1)z}{n+1}\), then \(n=\)
A. \(\dfrac{x-y+z}{z}\).
B. \(\dfrac{x+y-z}{z}\).
C. \(\dfrac{y-x-z}{x+z}\).
D. \(\dfrac{y-x-z}{x-z}\).
E. \(\dfrac{y+x-z}{x-z}\).

809. Solve the inequality \((4x+3)(x-4) \gt 0\)
A. \(x \gt 4\).
B. \(4 \gt x \gt -\dfrac{3}{4}\).
C. \(-\dfrac{3}{4} \gt x\).
D. \(-\dfrac{3}{4} \gt x \text{ or } x \gt 4\).
E. \(x \gt -\dfrac{3}{4}\).

8010. When the hour hand has turned through an angle \(x^\circ\), what is the angle through which the minute hand has turned?
A. \(6x^\circ\)
B. \(12x^\circ\)
C. \(60x^\circ\)
D. \(360x^\circ\)
E. \(3\,600x^\circ\)

8011. The first term of an arithmetic progression is \(6\) and its tenth term is three times its second term. The common difference is
A. \(18\).
B. \(4\).
C. \(3\).
D. \(2\).
E. \(1\).

8012. A man sold a car for \(\$35\,000\) at a loss of \(30\%\) on the cost price. What would have been the loss or gain percent if he had sold it for \(\$50\,500\)?
A. A gain of \(10\%\)
B. A gain of \(1\%\)
C. No gain nor loss
D. A loss of \(10\%\)
E. A loss of \(1\%\)

8013. If the length of a rectangle is increased by \(10\%\) and the width decreased by \(10\%\), which of the following is true?
A. Its area remains the same.
B. Its area is decreased by \(1\%\).
C. Its area is increased by \(1\%\).
D. Its area is decreased by \(10\%\).
E. Its area is increased by \(10\%\).

8014. The length of a side of a rhombus is \(10 \text{ cm}\). If its shorter diagonal is of length \(12 \text{ cm}\), what is the area of the rhombus in \(\text{ cm}^2\)?
A. \(60\)
B. \(96\)
C. \(100\)
D. \(120\)
E. \(192\)

8015. If the bearing of \(B\) from \(A\) is \(\text{S}30^\circ\text{W}\), then the bearing of \(A\) from \(B\) is
A. \(\text{N}30^\circ\text{E}\).
B. \(\text{N}60^\circ\text{W}\).
C. \(\text{N}60^\circ\text{E}\).
D. \(\text{S}30^\circ\text{W}\).
E. \(\text{S}30^\circ\text{E}\).

8016. \(\dfrac{1}{\dfrac{1}{\sin\theta}-1}-\dfrac{1}{\dfrac{1}{\sin\theta}+1}=\)
A. \(2\tan\theta\).
B. \(2\tan^2\theta\).
C. \(\dfrac{2}{\tan^2\theta}\).
D. \(2\sin\theta\).
E. \(\dfrac{\cos^2\theta}{2\sin^2\theta}\).

8017. If \(\cos\theta=x\) and \(0^\circ \lt \theta \lt 90^\circ\), then \(\tan\theta\)
A. \(\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}\).
B. \(\sqrt{1-x^2}\).
C. \(\dfrac{\sqrt{1-x^2}}{x}\).
D. \(\dfrac{x}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}\).
E. \(\pm\dfrac{x}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}\).

8018. If \(0^\circ\le\theta \lt 360^\circ\), which of the following equations has exactly one root?
A. \(\sin\theta=-1\)
B. \(\sin\theta=-\dfrac{1}{2}\)
C. \(\sin\theta=0\)
D. \(\sin\theta=\dfrac{1}{2}\)
E. \(\sin\theta=2\)

8019.
In the figure, \(a:b:c=\)
A. \(3:2:1\).
B. \(9:4:1\).
C. \(2:\sqrt{3}:1\).
D. \(\sqrt{3}:\sqrt{2}:1\).
E. \(\sqrt{3}:2:1\).

8020. What is the area, in \(\text{ cm}^2\), of an equilateral triangle of side \(x \text{ cm}\)?
A. \(\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{4}x^2\)
B. \(\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{2}x^2\)
C. \(\dfrac{1}{4}x^2\)
D. \(\dfrac{1}{2}x^2\)
E. \(\sqrt{3}x^2\)

8021.
In the figure, \(ABCDE\) is a regular pentagon. \(\angleADB=\)
A. \(35^\circ\).
B. \(36^\circ\).
C. \(40^\circ\).
D. \(54^\circ\).
E. \(72^\circ\).

8022.
In the figure, \(ABCD\) is a square with \(AB=5\). \(AP=BQ=CR=DS=1\). What is the area of \(PQRS\)?
A. \(9\)
B. \(15\)
C. \(16\)
D. \(17\)
E. \(18\)

8023.
In the figure, \(ABCD\) is a square and \(ABE\) is an equilateral triangle. \(\angleADE=?\)
A. \(72^\circ\).
B. \(74^\circ\).
C. \(76^\circ\).
D. \(78^\circ\).
E. None of the above.

8024.
In the figure, the two circles intersect at \(A\) and \(B\). \(CAE\) and \(CBD\) are straight lines. \(\angleCED=\)
A. \(y^\circ\).
B. \(180^\circ-y^\circ\).
C. \(180^\circ-x^\circ-y^\circ\).
D. \(180^\circ-x^\circ+y^\circ\).
E. \(360^\circ-x^\circ-y^\circ\).

8025.
In the figure, circle \(AXB\) passes through the centre of circle \(AYB\). \(y=\)
A. \(2x\).
B. \(180-2x\).
C. \(180-x\).
D. \(\dfrac{1}{2}(90-x)\).
E. \(\dfrac{1}{2}(180-x)\).

8026.
In the figure, \(ABCD\) is a rectangle \(\angleBEF=90^\circ\). Which two of the triangles I, II, III and IV must be similar?
A. I and II
B. I and III
C. II and III
D. II and IV
E. III and IV

8028. A certain sum of money is just sufficient to pay the wages of one man for \(m\) days or the wages of one boy for \(n\) days. For how many days will this sum be just sufficient to pay the wages of one man and one boy together?
A. \(m+n\)
B. \(\dfrac{m+n}{2}\)
C. \(\dfrac{1}{m}+\dfrac{1}{n}\)
D. \(\dfrac{m+n}{mn}\)
E. \(\dfrac{mn}{m+n}\)

8029. If the value of \(y^2+3y+7\) is \(2\), what is the value of \(2y^2+6y-3\)?
A. \(-13\)
B. \(-7\)
C. \(7\)
D. \(13\)
E. It cannot be found from the information given.

8030. \(A, B, C\) are three spheres. If \(\dfrac{\text{Surface area of } A}{\text{Surface area of } B}=4\) and \(\dfrac{\text{Volume of } B}{\text{Volume of } C}=2\), then \(\dfrac{\text{Volume of } A}{\text{Volume of } C}=\)
A. \(16\).
B. \(8\).
C. \(2\).
D. \(\dfrac{1}{8}\).
E. \(\dfrac{1}{16}\).

8031. (Refer to Question 30 options as per structure logic)
The figure above shows the graph of \(y=ax^2+bx+c\). Determine whether \(a\) and \(c\) are positive or negative.
A. \(a \gt 0\) and \(c \gt 0\)
B. \(a \lt 0\) and \(c \lt 0\)
C. \(a \gt 0\) and \(c \lt 0\)
D. \(a \lt 0\) and \(c \gt 0\)
E. It cannot be determined from the given data.

8032. \(P\) amounts to \(Q\) in \(n\) years at simple interest. The rate per annum is
A. \(\dfrac{100n(Q-P)}{P}\%\).
B. \(\dfrac{100(Q-P)}{n}\%\).
C. \(\dfrac{100(Q-P)}{nP}\%\).
D. \(\dfrac{100(Q-P)}{nQ}\%\).
E. \(100\left(\left(\dfrac{Q}{P}\right)^{\frac{1}{n}}-1\right)\%\).

8033. If \(0 \lt x \lt 1\), which of \(x, x^2, \dfrac{1}{x}, \sqrt{x}\) is the smallest? Which is the largest?
A. \(\sqrt{x}\) is smallest, \(x^2\) is largest
B. \(\dfrac{1}{x}\) is smallest, \(x^2\) is largest
C. \(x\) is smallest, \(\dfrac{1}{x}\) is largest
D. \(x^2\) is smallest, \(\dfrac{1}{x}\) is largest
E. \(x^2\) is smallest, \(\sqrt{x}\) is largest

8034. The Highest Common Factor of two unequal positive integers \(a\) and \(b\) is \(8\). Which of the following must be true?
I. The difference between \(a\) and \(b\) is divisible by \(8\)
II. \((a+b)\) is divisible by \(16\)
III. \(ab\) is divisible by \(64\)
A. III only
B. I and II only
C. I and III only
D. II and III only
E. I, II and III only

8035. \(x, y\) and \(z\) are three consecutive positive integers. Which of the following is true?
A. \(x+y+z\) must be odd
B. \(x+y+z\) must be even
C. \(xyz\) must be odd
D. \(xyz\) must be even
E. \(x^2+y^2+z^2\) must be even

8036. If \(x^2-kx+9\ge0\) for all real values of \(x\), what is the value of \(k\)?
A. \(k=-6\) only
B. \(k=6\) only
C. \(-6\le k\le6\)
D. \(k=6\) or \(-6\) only
E. \(k\le-6\) or \(k\ge6\)

8037. If \(x\) and \(y\) are real numbers, what is the minimum value of the expression \((x+y)^2-1\)?
A. \(-5\)
B. \(-1\)
C. \(0\)
D. \(3\)
E. It cannot be determined.

8038.
In the figure, the areas of the surfaces \(A, B, C\) of the cuboid are \(10 \text{ cm}^2\), \(14 \text{ cm}^2\) and \(35 \text{ cm}^2\) respectively. What is the volume of the cuboid?
A. \(49 \text{ cm}^3\)
B. \(70 \text{ cm}^3\)
C. \(140 \text{ cm}^3\)
D. \(350 \text{ cm}^3\)
E. \(4\,900 \text{ cm}^3\)

8039. \(x\) is a positive integer such that \(x^2+2x+7\) is even. What are the possible values of \(x\)?
A. \(x\) can be any positive integer
B. \(x\) can be any positive even number
C. \(x\) can be any positive odd number
D. \(x\) must be an even number greater than \(10\,000\)
E. \(x\) must be an positive odd number greater than \(10\,000\)

8040. The perimeter of a sector is \(16\) and its angle is \(2\) radians. What is the area of the sector?
A. \(16\)
B. \(32\)
C. \(64\)
D. \(16\pi\)
E. \(32\pi\)
